content: Phase I success criteria include: acceptable safety profile with manageable adverse events, defined maximum tolerated dose or recommended Phase II dose (RP2D), predictable pharmacokinetics supporting intended dosing frequency, and no unexpected severe toxicities. Dose-limiting toxicity (DLT) is typically defined as Grade 3+ adverse event related to study drug occurring in ≥33% of subjects at a dose level. Oncology Phase I trials use different endpoints (objective response rate, disease control rate) since they enroll patients rather than healthy volunteers. Approximately 70% of drugs pass Phase I and advance to Phase II.
